<planning_context>
**Phase:** C4
**Mode:** standard

<files_to_read>
- .planning/STATE.md (Project State)
- .planning/ROADMAP.md (Roadmap — line 1552 for C4 definition)
- .planning/REQUIREMENTS.md (Project Requirements)
- .planning/phases/C4-Performance-Optimization/C4-RESEARCH.md (Technical Research — already complete, HIGH confidence)
- .planning/phases/C4-Performance-Optimization/C4-SPEC.md (Specification — 4 low-ambiguity requirements, REQ-01 through REQ-04)
- .planning/phases/C4-Performance-Optimization/C4-VALIDATION.md (Validation Strategy)
</files_to_read>

**Phase description:** Fix 4 distinct Node.js performance issues across 4 source files — replacing synchronous/blocking patterns with async equivalents, adding a per-invocation cache for redundant JSON parsing, and preventing unbounded timer accumulation — without changing any public API surface or adding external dependencies.

**Phase requirement IDs (every ID MUST appear in a plan's `requirements` field):** REQ-01, REQ-02, REQ-03, REQ-04

**Project instructions:** Read ./AGENTS.md if exists — follow project-specific guidelines.

**TDD Mode is ENABLED.** Apply TDD heuristics:
- Business logic with defined I/O → type: tdd
- Data transformations, validation, algorithms → type: tdd
- UI, config, glue code, CRUD → standard plan (type: execute)
Each TDD plan gets one feature with RED/GREEN/REFACTOR gate sequence.

**No external packages needed** — all fixes use built-in Node.js APIs only (v26.0.0 confirmed).

**No public API changes allowed** — function signatures, export names, parameter shapes must remain unchanged.

## Requirements Summary

**REQ-01 (4.1):** Cache redundant JSON.parse in delegation-status.ts — Add per-invocation Map cache with TTL (5s, max 10 entries) keyed by `projectRoot::rootSessionId` to avoid parsing `hierarchy-manifest.json` more than once per tool execution.

**REQ-02 (4.2):** Convert sync FS to async in bootstrap-init tool path — Replace mkdirSync/writeFileSync/readFileSync with fs.promises equivalents in tool-exposed bootstrapInit path. CLI-only helpers (readInstalledPackageVersion, shouldRefreshSchemaArtifact) remain sync.

**REQ-03 (4.3):** Add pruneStaleTimers in CompletionDetector — Add method that iterates stabilityTimers Map, clears timers older than maxAgeMs, removes from stabilityTimers, messageCounts, and a new timerStartTimes Map. Returns count of pruned timers.

**REQ-04 (4.4):** Replace execSync with async execFile in create-governance-session.ts — Split git commands into execFileAsync("git", ["add", "-A"]) and execFileAsync("git", ["commit", ...]). Best-effort semantics preserved (empty catch on failure).

## Constraints
- No external packages — all fixes use built-in Node.js APIs only (node:fs/promises, node:child_process, node:util)
- No public API changes — function signatures, export names, and parameter shapes must remain unchanged
- Node.js >= 20.0.0 (v26.0.0 available)
- Best-effort semantics preserved — empty catch blocks must not propagate failures to callers
- CLI bootstrap path must remain usable — only tool handler path needs async

## Architecture Patterns
- **Pattern 1:** Per-invocation Map cache with TTL (5s, max 10 entries) for JSON.parse results
- **Pattern 2:** Sync-to-async FS wrapper — replace mkdirSync/writeFileSync/readFileSync with fs.promises equivalents
- **Pattern 3:** Timer cleanup — pruneStaleTimers method that iterates stabilityTimers and removes stale entries
- **Pattern 4:** execFileAsync — split git commands into separate execFileAsync calls (binary, args) pattern

## Files to modify
- src/coordination/delegation/delegation-status.ts (REQ-01)
- src/tools/config/bootstrap-init.ts (REQ-02)
- src/coordination/completion/detector.ts (REQ-03)
- src/features/governance-engine/create-governance-session.ts (REQ-04)

## Existing test files
- tests/tools/delegation/delegation-status-v2.test.ts (257 LOC)
- tests/tools/delegation-status.test.ts (571 LOC)
- tests/tools/bootstrap-init.test.ts (210 LOC)
- tests/lib/coordination/completion/detector-v2.test.ts (100 LOC)
- tests/features/governance-engine/create-governance-session.test.ts (425 LOC)

## New test files needed (Wave 0)
- tests/coordination/completion/detector-stability-prune.test.ts — covers pruneStaleTimers (REQ-03)

</planning_context>

<downstream_consumer>
Output consumed by /gsd-execute-phase. Plans need:
- Frontmatter (wave, depends_on, files_modified, autonomous)
- Tasks in XML format with read_first and acceptance_criteria fields (MANDATORY on every task)
- Verification criteria
- must_haves for goal-backward verification
</downstream_consumer>

<deep_work_rules>
## Anti-Shallow Execution Rules (MANDATORY)

Every task MUST include these fields:
1. **`<read_first>`** — Files the executor MUST read before touching anything
2. **`<acceptance_criteria>`** — Verifiable conditions that prove the task was done correctly
3. **`<action>`** — Must include CONCRETE values, not references

**Why this matters:** Executor agents work from the plan text. Vague instructions like "update the config to match production" produce shallow one-line changes.
</deep_work_rules>

<quality_gate>
- [ ] PLAN.md files created in phase directory
- [ ] Each plan has valid frontmatter
- [ ] Tasks are specific and actionable
- [ ] Every task has `<read_first>` with at least the file being modified
- [ ] Every task has `<acceptance_criteria>` with behavior, test-command, CLI, or source assertions
- [ ] Every `<action>` contains concrete identifiers without fenced code blocks or full implementations
- [ ] Dependencies correctly identified
- [ ] Waves assigned for parallel execution
- [ ] must_haves derived from phase goal
</quality_gate>
